title: Jay Baruchel's Directorial Debut and Making an Authentic Hockey Movie
format: Katie Baker walks Sean Fennessey through the great hockey movies, then Jay Baruchel on directing Goon: Last of the Enforcers.

films.title: Goon
films.note: Sean William Scott as a bar bouncer who can't skate but can fight his way to a minor-league enforcer job. Baruchel co-wrote it.

films.title: Slap Shot
films.note: Baker's consensus No. 1; she rewatched it and found it better than she remembered.

films.title: Miracle
films.note: The 1980 Miracle on Ice team; a locker-room speech Baker rates against Al Pacino's.

films.title: Sudden Death
films.note: Van Damme at a Penguins game, including a fight with the mascot in the arena kitchen. Baker's 5b.

films.title: Youngblood
films.note: A snapshot of a certain era; the Bill Simmons generation of hockey movie.

films.title: The Mighty Ducks
films.note: Not the best but arguably the most important hockey movie, since it launched an actual NHL franchise.

films.title: D2: The Mighty Ducks
films.note: Where the Goldberg-vs-Julie 'the Cat' Gaffney tension pays off.

films.title: Little Giants
films.note: Cited alongside the '90s kids-sports run for Becky 'the Icebox'.

films.title: The Sandlot
films.note: Grouped with Little Giants as the era's good kids-sports movies.

films.title: Any Given Sunday
films.note: The Pacino speech Miracle gets measured against.

segments.heading: Jay Baruchel on his directorial debut

films.title: Goon: Last of the Enforcers
films.note: Baruchel's first feature as director. Where the original was about falling in love, the sequel is about an aging enforcer married to a job that's a finite, damaging thing.

films.title: Goon
films.note: Baruchel co-wrote it with Evan Goldberg; he made Doug Jewish to route the story through his own father's hockey-fighting past.

films.title: Random Acts of Violence
films.note: The horror adaptation of his and Jesse Chabot's graphic novel, which he was hoping to direct next.

How to Train Your Dragon.title: How to Train Your Dragon: The Hidden World
